subject: Chemistry
solution:
An odd electron bond means there is an odd number of the electron in the overall molecule. Or we can say that the molecule has unpaired electrons.

So, in NO, the valence electrons in nitrogen are 5, and valence electrons in oxygen are 6. So, by adding we get, 6+5=11

In sulfuric acid (H2SO4), each oxygen has a full octet (eight valence electrons), whereas sulfur has an expanded octet (twelve valence electrons).

(A) BCl3→ Even Electron molecule SF6→ Expanded octet molecule

(B) NO→ Odd Electron molecule H2SO4→ Expanded octet.

(C) SF6→ Even Electron molecule H2SO4→ Expanded octet.

(D) BCl3→ Even Electron molecule NO→ Odd Electron molecule

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Number of hybrid orbitals = Number of bond pair + number of lone pair on central atom.

To have sp3d2 hybridisation there should be total of 6 (bond pairs + lone pairs). The structure in such case will be octahedral or its derivative structures.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The bond order of a diatomic species can be calculated using molecular orbital theory as follows,

Bond order=No.of BMO electrons-No.of ABMO electrons2

BMO = bonding molecular orbital

ABMO = Anti bonding molecular orbital.

Based on the total number electrons in bond orders are given in the following table.

Number of e- Bond order
13 2.5
14 3
15 2.5
16 2

CN- , NO+ and O22+ are having 14 electrons each, hence the bond order is 3.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Li 2 - < Li 2 + < Li 2
Bond order ∝ stability of species
Li2=σ1s2<σ*1s2<σ2s2                      ;  B.O=4−22=1
Li2−=σ1s2<σ*1s2<σ2s2<σ*2s1                      ;  B.O=4−32=0.5
Li2+=σ1s2<σ*1s2<σ2s1                      ;  B.O=3−22=0.5
Li2+ and Li2- both have same bond order, but Li2- has more number of electron in anti-bonding molecular orbitals (experience more inter electronic repulsion) and hence least stable among three species.

solution:
The molecular formulas for ammonia and phosphine are NH3 and  PH3
, respectively. As we know, Nitrogen is more electronegative and smaller in size than phosphorus. Therefore, hydrogen bonding occurs in ammonia in comparison to phosphine. Hydrogen bonding is the force of attraction between hydrogen and an electronegative element which is smaller in size. In the case of phosphine, only weak van der waal’s forces of attraction exist between the molecules. Since hydrogen bonding is stronger than weak van der waal’s forces of attraction. Hence, ammonia has a higher boiling point than phosphine.
In all, we can say that hydrogen bonding is responsible for the higher boiling point of ammonia in comparison to phosphine.

N has small atomic size and high electronegativity. Hence, NH3 can form hydrogen bonds. P has large size and low electronegativity. Hence, PH3 cannot form hydrogen bonds.

O 2 =σ1 s 2 < σ * 1 s 2 <σ2 s 2 < σ * 2 s 2 <σ2 p x 2 <π2 p y 2 =π2 p z 2 < π * 2 p y 1 = π * 2 p z 1

Bond order = 6−2 2 =2

N 2 =σ1 s 2 < σ * 1 s 2 <σ2 s 2 < σ * 2 s 2 <π2 p z 2 =π2 p z 2 <σ2 p x 2

Bond order = 6−0 2 =3

According to MOT ; electronic configuration of N2+ is:

N 2 + =σ1 s 2 < σ * 1 s 2 <σ2 s 2 < σ * 2 s 2 <π2 p x 2 =π2 p y 2 <σ2 p z 1

Bond order =129-4=2.5

i.e., 2π bonds and 0.5 σ bonds.

solution: Chlorine pentafluoride is an inter halogen compound. ClF5 is colourless liquid. The central chlorine atom is having five bond pairs and one lone pair on it. Hence, according to VSEPR theory it has square pyramidal geometry. It is toxic by inhalation and an irritant to skin, eyes and mucus membranes.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
In inorganic chemistry, Fajans' rules, formulated by Kazimierz Fajans in 1923, are used to predict whether a chemical bond will be covalent or ionic, and depend on the charge on the cation and the relative sizes of the cation and anion. They can be summarised in the following table:


$\begin{array}{|l|l|} \hline \text{Ionic } & \text{Covalent } \\ \hline \text{Low positive charge on cation} & \text{High positive charge on cation} \\ \hline \text{Large cation} & \text{Small cation} \\ \hline \text{Small anion} & \text{Large anion} \\ \hline \end{array}$

In the given case all the anions are same in all the given compounds. So we need to think about cations.

The ionic radius of Ag+ is less than that of Cl− but their charges are equal. Hence, AgCl is a least ionic compound among the given options.

AgCl<CoCl2<BaCl2<KCl (ionic character)

subject: Chemistry
options: London dispersion, dipole-dipole and hydrogen bonding
solution:
Ethyl acetate is polar molecule so dipole-dipole interaction will be present there. Also, hydrogen is not attached to electronegative element so, hydrogen bonding is not possible.

Hydrogen bonding, interaction involving a hydrogen atom located between a pair of other atoms having a high affinity for electrons; such a bond is weaker than an ionic bond or covalent bond but stronger than van der Waals forces.

solution:
Butan-1-ol can undergo hydrogen bonding due to the presence of an -OH group, ethoxyethane (also known as diethyl ether) can also participate in hydrogen bonding.

Even though there are no hydrogen atoms directly bonded to a highly electronegative atom (like F, O, or N) in ethoxyethane, the molecule still has polar C-O bonds that can lead to dipole-dipole interactions with other polar molecules.

Owing to intermolecular hydrogen bonding in butanol, it has higher boiling point than ethoxyethane.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
(i) ion-ion interaction energy is inversely proportional to the distance between ions 1r
(ii) dipole-dipole interaction energy is inversely proportional to the third power of r1r3
(iii) The interaction energy of London force is inversely proportional to sixth power of distance between two interaction particles 1r6

solution:
The molecule SO2 is having two sigma bond pairs and lone pair, hence, it exhibits angular or V-shape structure. The molecule H2O is having two sigma bond pairs and a lone pair hence, it exhibits angular or V-shape structure. So, both molecules are having same shape. But double bond-double bond repulsions are greater than single bond repulsions, hence, the bond angle in sulphur dioxide is greater than water.

SO2 and H2O both have V-Shape 

Bond angle: SO2 > H2O

subject: Chemistry
solution:
(i) Molecular orbital configuration of O22- is KK (σ2s)2 (σ*2s)2 𝜋(2px)2 𝜋(2py)2 (σ2pz)2 𝜋*(2px)2 𝜋*(2py)2

Bond order = 12 (Nb - Na) = 12 8 - 6 = 1

(ii) Molecular orbital configuration of CO is KK (σ2s)2 (σ*2s)2 𝜋(2px)2 𝜋(2py)2 (σ2pz)2 

Bond order = 12 (Nb - Na) = 12 8 - 2 = 3

(iii) Molecular orbital configuration of NO+ is KK (σ2s)2 (σ*2s)2 𝜋(2px)2 𝜋(2py)2 (σ2pz)2 

Bond order = 12 (Nb - Na) = 12 8 - 2 = 3

So, according to molecular orbital theory, the bond orders respectively are 1, 3 and 3.
